pr.title: Use symbol names from Zoids VS debug elf
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Renamed a small set of anonymous assembly labels/functions to real SDK/MSL-style symbol names taken from the Zoids VS debug ELF. The changes are almost entirely symbol-only updates in asm files, improving cross-file call readability for DVD, OS exception, audio, VI, reboot, serial, MSL printf, and one Melee callsite without changing behavior.
what_changed: Renamed MSL printf function func_80323E38 to vprintf and updated OSError.s callsites to call vprintf.
Renamed DVD functions func_80339BF8 to DVDSetAutoInvalidation, func_80339F50 to DVDCheckDisk, and func_8033A034 to __DVDPrepareResetAsync.
Updated OSReboot.s to call DVDSetAutoInvalidation, __DVDPrepareResetAsync, and DVDCheckDisk instead of address-style function names.
Renamed an OSReboot local callback label from lbl_80348138 to Callback and updated its address load.
Renamed VI function func_80350294 to VIGetCurrentLine and updated OSSerial.s to call VIGetCurrentLine.
Renamed OS exception-vector internal labels lbl_8034363C, lbl_8034364C, and lbl_8034367C to __DBVECTOR, __OSEVSetNumber, and __OSEVEnd, and updated references inside OSExceptionInit.
Renamed OSAudioSystem data label lbl_80401B70 to DSPInitCode and updated the memcpy source reference.
Updated one Melee assembly callsite in asm/melee/text_2.s from func_80339F50 to DVDCheckDisk.
Removed .global declarations from several internal branch labels inside DVDCheckDisk, indicating they should remain local labels rather than exported symbols.
smart_moves: Used an external debug ELF as evidence for real symbol names instead of guessing names from behavior alone.
Propagated renamed symbols to all visible assembly callsites in other files, preserving cross-file consistency.
Kept the PR narrowly scoped to symbol naming, making the diff low-risk and easy to review.
Distinguished public or SDK-like names such as DVDCheckDisk and VIGetCurrentLine from double-underscore internal symbols such as __DVDPrepareResetAsync and __OSEVSetNumber.
Cleaned up accidental global exposure of internal switch/branch labels in dvd.s while applying the symbol rename.
decomp_lessons: When a trusted debug ELF provides names, prefer those names over placeholder func_ or lbl_ symbols even before the surrounding code is fully decompiled.
A symbol rename in assembly must include all direct branch/call references and address materializations using @ha/@l relocation pairs.
SDK symbol naming can improve future C porting because callsites immediately reveal subsystem intent, such as DVD reset preparation or VI current-line queries.
Internal exception-vector labels may have canonical double-underscore names and should not necessarily be treated as generic local labels.
Data labels can be as important as functions: naming DSPInitCode clarifies the purpose of the copied audio initialization blob.
Avoid marking internal control-flow labels as .global unless there is evidence they are externally referenced symbols.
naming_conventions: Use official SDK-style PascalCase or camel-with-prefix names for Dolphin APIs: DVDSetAutoInvalidation, DVDCheckDisk, VIGetCurrentLine.
Use double-underscore prefixes for internal SDK/private symbols: __DVDPrepareResetAsync, __DBVECTOR, __OSEVSetNumber, __OSEVEnd.
Use meaningful data object names for known blobs, e.g. DSPInitCode instead of lbl_80401B70.
Simple local callback labels may be named descriptively as Callback when evidence supports that label from the source/debug symbols.
Replace func_<address> and lbl_<address> placeholders when external symbol evidence identifies the original name.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: For assembly-only symbol renames, update both the .global directive and the label definition together.
For address loads, update both @ha and @l relocation operands to the new symbol name, as seen for __OSEVSetNumber, __OSEVEnd, __DBVECTOR, Callback, and DSPInitCode.
For branch-and-link instructions, replace the target comment/symbol with the renamed function while leaving opcodes unchanged.
Do not change instruction bytes or control flow when the goal is symbol recovery; this PR is a behavior-preserving rename patch.
Remove .global annotations from labels that are internal to a function/control-flow region when they are not true exported symbols.
review_feedback: No issue comments or review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR was merged quickly after creation, suggesting the naming-only changes were accepted, but the slice does not include reviewer rationale.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that decompile or move DVDCheckDisk, DVDSetAutoInvalidation, or __DVDPrepareResetAsync into C.
Search for remaining func_/lbl_ callsites that correspond to symbols in the Zoids VS debug ELF.
Check whether DSPInitCode is later declared in a header or C translation unit with the same name.
Look for additional OS exception-vector labels that should use official SDK double-underscore names.
Verify if Callback in OSReboot.s remains sufficiently unique or is later renamed to a file-local/static-style name.
